title: Structural Cognitive Emergence (SCE): Toward Structural Cognitive Runtime and Brain-Unit Intelligence
description: Structural Cognitive Emergence (SCE) investigates how structural cognition may emerge from sparse examples, structural gaps, and developmental feedback rather than from brute-force memorization or purely black-box pattern fitting. The repository studies sparse concept formation, gap-driven why-question generation, in-situ structural knowledge growth, and a three-layer structural substrate built on Calling Graphs (CG), Differential Trees (DT), and Triggering Links (TL). This release extends SCE into its runtime phase through a new line of work on Structural Cognitive Runtime (SCR), including SCE-014, SCE-015, and SCE-016. In particular, SCE-016 proposes SCR as a runtime architecture for ASI Brain Units and Brain Unit Core, with explicit layers for structural memory, structural localization, sparse concept formation, Gap/Why generation, developmental structural update, and decision/action/escalation. The release also clarifies the distinction between front-end Brain Units and a larger structural backend, and discusses how LLMs may function as language, coding, hypothesis, simulation, and explanation engines inside a broader structural runtime.
